title: Genesis: Abraham Called
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: abraham called and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: abraham called and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What three things did God promise Abram when he called him in Genesis 12:1-3? Answer: a great nation, a great name, and that through him all families of the earth would be blessed.
segments.correct_answer: a great nation, a great name, and blessing to all families of the earth
acceptable_variants: land, seed, and blessing to all nations
descendants, fame, and that all peoples would be blessed through him

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: God's unconditional promise to Abram of land, descendants, and worldwide blessing; the foundational covenant of redemptive history
God's sovereign summons to Abram in Genesis 12 to leave his country for a land God would show him; the beginning of the covenant nation
